task_completion.description: Downloaded at least two relevant PDF guidance documents from the FDA website.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager showing the 'Downloads' folder (or browser download history) containing at least two PDF files with names indicating they are FDA guidance documents.

task_completion.description: Created a Favorites folder named 'FDA Guidance' in Microsoft Edge.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Microsoft Edge browser open with the Favorites bar or Favorites menu visible, showing a folder explicitly named 'FDA Guidance'.

task_completion.description: Bookmarked the consulted FDA pages within the 'FDA Guidance' Favorites folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the 'FDA Guidance' Favorites folder in Microsoft Edge are visible, showing at least two saved bookmarks pointing to FDA web pages.

task_completion.description: Created a regulatory research summary file named 'fda_research_summary.txt' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'fda_research_summary.txt' is visible on the Desktop directory, or open in a text editor with the file path '/home/ga/Desktop/fda_research_summary.txt'.

task_completion.description: The summary file includes the title, FDA URL or document identifier, and a one-sentence scope description for at least two guidance documents.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'fda_research_summary.txt' are visible in a text editor or terminal, displaying at least two distinct entries. Each entry must clearly show a document title, a URL (or ID), and a short sentence describing its scope.

integrity.description: The agent actually used Microsoft Edge to navigate the FDA website and create bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Microsoft Edge GUI open, navigating to fda.gov, and interacting with the Favorites menu, rather than bookmarks being injected via terminal scripts.

integrity.description: The agent downloaded actual PDF files rather than creating empty dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The downloaded PDF files show realistic file sizes in the file manager, or are seen opened in a PDF viewer displaying genuine FDA document formatting.

integrity.description: The URLs provided in the summary file are genuine FDA URLs and not hallucinated.
integrity.visual_evidence: The URLs written in 'fda_research_summary.txt' start with 'https://www.fda.gov/' and match the pages the agent was seen visiting.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Valid FDA guidance documents are hosted on fda.gov. The task involves finding guidance documents relevant to a monoclonal antibody for rheumatoid arthritis (NDA preparation). Examples of relevant real FDA guidance documents include: (1) Pharmacokinetics in Patients With Impaired Renal Function, (2) Bioavailability and Bioequivalence Studies Submitted in NDAs or INDs, (3) Food-Effect Bioavailability and Fed Bioequivalence Studies, (4) ICH E5 Ethnic Factors in the Acceptability of Foreign Clinical Data. All referenced regulatory terms (NDA, BLA, DMARD, pharmacokinetics) are authentic.
